title: dianthus rupicola subsp. bocchoriana
intro: Dianthus rupicola subsp. bocchoriana, also known as the 'Alpine Pink', is a rare subspecies of the Dianthus rupicola plant, native to the mountains of Crete. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dianthus rupicola subsp. bocchoriana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including colds and respiratory issues.
